birch.name: Birch
birch.meaning: The pioneer — new beginnings, purification, first light after the longest night
birch.symbol: The first tree to colonize cleared ground — arriving where nothing else yet grows
birch.shadow: The compulsive new beginning that avoids ever completing. Starting over when the real work is deepening. Mistaking fresh starts for growth.
birch.gift: The genuine capacity to begin — not from naivety, but from a clean relationship with possibility. You colonize the empty ground before others dare arrive.
birch.primeInsight: You are the tree that arrives first when everything has been burned. Your gift is not just new beginnings but the courage required when the ground is still cold and the outcome unknown.
birch.primeSelfAlignment: Your Forge works best at initiations and genuine new chapters. Invite Birch energy whenever you must begin something with no guarantees.

rowan.name: Rowan
rowan.meaning: The protector — psychic defense, discernment against deception, foresight
rowan.symbol: Mountains and high places; red berries that ward against enchantment
rowan.shadow: Hyper-vigilant protection that sees danger everywhere and allows no one close. Discernment turned into perpetual suspicion.
rowan.gift: An almost uncanny ability to perceive falsehood and enchantment. You see through manipulation, crowd hysteria, and false appearances in ways others cannot.
rowan.primeInsight: You carry natural wards against deception — psychic, emotional, environmental. This is not paranoia; it is perception. The challenge is trusting it without becoming isolated by it.
rowan.primeSelfAlignment: When you need to see through confusion or deception — in relationships, organizations, or your own narrative — this is your tree of clarity.

ash.name: Ash
ash.meaning: The world-tree — the axis connecting the three worlds, expansive consciousness
ash.symbol: Yggdrasil — the world tree whose roots reach the underworld and whose crown touches the heavens
ash.shadow: Expansive consciousness without grounding — thoughts and visions that circle endlessly without landing in action. Sacrifice made compulsively rather than chosen with understanding.
ash.gift: The natural bridge between worlds — between the practical and spiritual, the conscious and unconscious, the individual and collective. Ash-born people synthesize what others keep separate.
ash.primeInsight: Odin hung on the world-tree to gain the runes — sacrificing comfort for the wisdom that changes everything. Your path includes voluntary sacrifice for genuine understanding. The returns are extraordinary.
ash.primeSelfAlignment: When you need to integrate opposites, cross between domains, or make the synthesis that unlocks a new level of understanding — call on this tree's capacity.

alder.name: Alder
alder.meaning: The pathfinder — the counselor who bridges the temporal and the spiritual
alder.ruler: Bran the Blessed; Mars; the Spirit of the Forest
alder.symbol: The tree that thrives at the water's edge — bridging the realms of water and land
alder.shadow: Using oracular capacity for personal power or control. Courage turned into recklessness. Bridging that serves the bridge-maker's needs more than those crossing.
alder.gift: The gift of genuine counsel — reading situations across multiple dimensions simultaneously and giving guidance that is practical and spiritually accurate at once.
alder.primeInsight: Alder grows where others cannot — at the edge between water and land, the threshold between practical and spiritual. You are built for these liminal zones. Your counsel is most valuable in exactly the situations where others have no map.
alder.primeSelfAlignment: When navigating major decisions requiring both pragmatic and intuitive intelligence — this is your tree. Also invoke when serving as a true counselor to others.

willow.name: Willow
willow.meaning: The dreamer — lunar cycles, the deep feminine, psychic receptivity, rhythm of the unconscious
willow.symbol: The tree that bends without breaking; roots always at the water's edge
willow.shadow: Flexibility without core — bending to every wind until there is no longer a self. Emotional depth that becomes drowning. Lunar sensitivity without solar grounding.
willow.gift: Extraordinary receptivity to the emotional and psychic undercurrents of any person or situation. Connection to the cyclical nature of reality — understanding timing through feeling rather than calculation.
willow.primeInsight: You cannot be broken because you bend. But bending is only the shadow of your gift — the deeper truth is that your root goes all the way to the water table, to the unconscious, to the source. You are flexible because you are well-rooted.
willow.primeSelfAlignment: In times requiring deep listening, emotional intelligence, or reading the timing of tidal forces — this is your tree. Invite Willow in any work with the unconscious or with grief.

hawthorn.name: Hawthorn
hawthorn.meaning: The purifier — the guardian tree, cleansing of the heart before union, sacred paradox
hawthorn.symbol: Thorns and blossoms on the same branch; guardian of the fairy realm
hawthorn.shadow: The thorn without the blossom — protection so fierce that nothing can enter, including love. Using purity as a weapon against others or self.
hawthorn.gift: The capacity to hold sacred paradox — the thorn and the blossom are the same branch. You understand that genuine beauty requires genuine protection, and true love requires real discernment about who enters your sacred space.
hawthorn.primeInsight: The hawthorn marks the threshold of the sacred — what stands behind it is worth the challenge of getting past the thorns. Your function is to protect what is most essentially valuable while remaining fully alive to beauty.
hawthorn.primeSelfAlignment: In matters of sacred relationships, creative thresholds, or the purification required before a genuine new beginning — Hawthorn is your guide.

oak.name: Oak
oak.meaning: The king — strength, endurance, sovereignty, the door to the mysteries
oak.symbol: The king of trees; the lightning-struck oak that still stands; the Druid's sacred tree
oak.shadow: Rigidity masquerading as strength. The king who cannot learn from the young. Hoarding rather than the sovereign's generosity. Overconfidence in one's own judgment.
oak.gift: The oak does not grow fast — it grows for centuries. Your strength is of the same order: genuine, tested, enduring. When the winter comes, your roots were already that deep.
oak.primeInsight: You are not built for speed — you are built for duration. The strength you embody, when authentic, becomes a landmark others navigate by. This is not about power over others; this is about becoming a permanent resource.
oak.primeSelfAlignment: When endurance, structural integrity, or genuine sovereignty is required — Oak is your foundational tree. The Druidic orders recognized it as the axis of the sacred world.

holly.name: Holly
holly.meaning: The warrior — the twin of oak, the holly king who rules the waning year
holly.symbol: The only tree that challenged the oak and won — green through winter, red berries in the cold
holly.shadow: The warrior who has forgotten what they fight for. Combativeness without purpose. Endurance without learning. The knight who only knows battle.
holly.gift: The specific gift of remaining vital and green when everything else has gone dormant. A natural capacity for functioning at your best under exactly the conditions that deplete others.
holly.primeInsight: Holly blooms when the Oak King falls — not in conquest, but in the natural rotation of what the world needs. Your strength is not acquired in prosperity; it was forged in winters others needed to survive.
holly.primeSelfAlignment: When facing trials that seem designed to break you — Holly is your archetype. This is the tree of the initiation that proves you. Call it in when the season has turned cold.

hazel.name: Hazel
hazel.meaning: The seer — wisdom, inspiration, poetic knowledge, sacred knowledge through direct vision
hazel.ruler: Fintan mac Bóchra (the Salmon of Wisdom); Mercury; Sun
hazel.symbol: The nine hazel trees above the sacred pool; the Salmon of Knowledge
hazel.shadow: Knowledge hoarded rather than shared. Using the gift of vision for personal advantage. The inspired seer who never acts on their visions.
hazel.gift: Direct access to wisdom that cannot be reached by linear reasoning — the salmon swims to the hazel pool and receives the complete knowledge. You receive insight whole rather than assembled.
hazel.primeInsight: The Salmon of Wisdom ate the hazel nuts that fell from the tree of knowledge. Your knowing often arrives exactly this way — sudden, complete, not requiring a chain of logic. Trust this. It is genuine perception, not intuitive performance.
hazel.primeSelfAlignment: The Intel archetype and the wisdom-keeper. When raw insight, creative inspiration, or the location of hidden truth is needed — this is your tree. Hazel dowses for what is real beneath the surface.

vine.name: Vine
vine.meaning: The paradox-bearer — prophecy through intoxication, harvest, truth extracted from fermentation
vine.symbol: The harvest vine; that which makes boundaries dissolve; the fruit that ferments into revelation
vine.shadow: Intoxication as avoidance — using the dissolution of boundaries to escape rather than to penetrate to truth. Harvest taken without having done the planting.
vine.gift: The specific kind of wisdom that comes when the rational guard is lowered — not through unconsciousness, but through the allowing that comes after harvest. Your insights often arrive through surrender rather than effort.
vine.primeInsight: Vine-born individuals are designed for the moment of 'enough' — when the harvest is taken, the work is complete, and truth can be celebrated rather than pursued. You teach others that pleasure and truth are not opposites.
vine.primeSelfAlignment: When the rational approach has failed and what's needed is a different mode of knowing — Vine. Also: when the work is genuinely complete and celebration is the honest response.

ivy.name: Ivy
ivy.meaning: The perseverer — the spiral path, tenacious soul-seeking, the eternal quest
ivy.symbol: The spiral; the plant that cannot be stopped by walls — climbing, adapting, persisting
ivy.shadow: Clinging where it is not welcome; persistence that becomes parasitic; growth that chokes rather than companions.
ivy.gift: The capacity to grow in any direction required, along any surface available, without losing contact with the root. Ivy-born individuals cannot be stopped by ordinary obstacles — they route around them.
ivy.primeInsight: The spiral path is not inefficiency — it is the only path that integrates all the necessary terrain. You return to the same questions at greater depth, and each return is genuine progress even when it looks like the same ground.
ivy.primeSelfAlignment: In long pilgrimages — the spiritual kind, and the literal kind. When the path winds through dark territory and the question is whether to continue or turn back.

reed.name: Reed
reed.meaning: The messenger — the voice of the otherworld, keeper of truth-in-death, the threshold guide
reed.symbol: The cut reed that becomes the musical instrument — the hollow through which the wind of the otherworld speaks
reed.shadow: Becoming the hollow instrument without a voice of your own. Channeling the dead rather than the living. Making every moment a threshold and every space a sacred chamber — never a house.
reed.gift: The gift of the reed flute: you were cut from your grounding by loss or challenge, and that cutting is what enables you to make music. The wound is the opening through which wisdom speaks.
reed.primeInsight: Like the reed in Rumi's poem — the longing in your voice is what carries the true message. Your experience of separation from what mattered most is not a loss: it is the instrument through which you transmit something irreplaceable.
reed.primeSelfAlignment: In initiatory death-and-rebirth passages. When communicating difficult truths. When guiding others through thresholds of loss. Reed-time is for serious work.

elder.name: Elder
elder.meaning: The completion — the wise elder at the year's end, the crone goddess, all-uses medicine
elder.ruler: The Cailleach; Saturn; the Dark Goddess
elder.symbol: All parts of the Elder useful for medicine; the tree at the threshold before winter silence
elder.shadow: White Elder witch — the wisdom turned toward manipulation or the hoarding of power. Completeness turned into cynicism or refusal to mentor the next generation.
elder.gift: Nothing is wasted. Every part of the Elder serves a purpose — bark, flower, berry, leaf. Elder-born individuals carry an extraordinary capacity to transmute all experience into useful wisdom, including the darkest.
elder.primeInsight: You stand at the end of the year knowing what the year cost and what it produced. The Elder wisdom is this: nothing was wasted. The completeness you embody includes the falls, the failures, the losses — all of it metabolized into medicine.
elder.primeSelfAlignment: When arriving at genuine completion — of a chapter, a learning, a relationship's form. Elder wisdom governs the art of ending well and preparing what must be carried across into the new cycle.

nameless.name: The Nameless Day
nameless.meaning: The day outside time — the unlived zone between the death of the old year and the birth of the new
nameless.ruler: No deity claims this day; it belongs to the uncreated
nameless.symbol: Absolute silence; the pregnant void before the first word
nameless.shadow: Terror of the formless — the anxiety that arises in the space between structures, needing immediately to categorize and contain.
nameless.gift: An unusual ease with paradox, the undefined, and the space between categories. Those born on this day carry a kind of freedom from the normal structures that govern identity.
nameless.primeInsight: You were born in the interstitial — the crack between worlds. This is not a misfortune. It is a specific assignment: to know what is possible when the usual rules are suspended. You are the embodied exception.
nameless.primeSelfAlignment: When all maps have run out and the only navigation is direct perception of what is — this is your territory. The day between trees belongs to those who can function in the uncharted.
